utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: I want to just say thank you to the chair for being a fighter.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And whatever questions, I'm happy to have a follow-up conversation with you just so you can get the answer.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: We'll be sending it over.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: All right.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Great.

chapters.summary: Lee asks about MOIA's involvement with the World Cup and encouraging exploration of immigrant neighborhoods. Ali describes the NYC Neighborhood Passport program launching June 11th, encouraging New Yorkers to explore five boroughs and collect artist-designed stamps from community organizations.
chapters.title: Chair Lee questions MOIA on FIFA World Cup and NYC Neighborhood Passport

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Actually, since we have like two minutes, one final question that's more fun about the World Cup that's happening right now this year.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: But just out of curiosity, because we have so many great small businesses, immigrant-owned parts of our city, especially Queens.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: I mean, I think the Queens council members fight over who has the better food in their district.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: But we know that this is coming up.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And so just was curious to know, I know that the mayor's office is launching New York City Neighborhood.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And so are you guys working with them at all to encourage folks to explore a lot of different neighborhoods in the city and get out there and explore?

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: I'm glad to end on a fun note.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Yeah, the New York City Neighborhood Passport is a citywide program.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: It launches on June 11th.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And the idea is to encourage New Yorkers to visit and explore the five boroughs and many of the immigrant neighborhoods that make this city so great.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Participants are collecting artist-designed stamps from hundreds of community organizations and cultural institutions and public events, from dance performances and film screenings.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: It's really a great opportunity for the community to get involved and explore our great city.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Great.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And if you want recommendations from all of us in terms of which restaurants are the best in our area, let us know.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Add it to a list.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We'll be sending that out.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay.

chapters.summary: Lee thanks the Commissioner and team, reminds attendees about the June 10th public testimony date, and concludes the third day of hearings covering ACS, DYCD, and MOIA.
chapters.title: Chair Lee concludes three days of FY27 executive budget hearings

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And I think with that, we are done for the day.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: So thank you so much, Commissioner, and everyone else for being here.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Oh.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Just to remind them about the public hearings.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Oh, yeah.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: So just, again, public hearing Wednesday, June 10th.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Make sure everyone signs up.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: So thank you so much, everyone.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: And that concludes our third day of hearings.

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: Thank you, Chair.

summary: NYC Council Executive Budget Hearing for FY27 covering ACS, DYCD, and MOIA, with Chairs Linda Lee and Althea Stevens leading questioning.
ACS Commissioner Rebecca Jones-Gaston testified on $3.51B budget, savings measures, prevention service cuts, child care vouchers, juvenile detention, and Fair Futures program.
DYCD Commissioner Sandra Escamilla-Davies faced extensive questioning on the Compass RFP process, provider transitions, Summer Rising, SYEP, and savings targets.
MOIA Commissioner Faiza Ali testified on immigration legal services, ICE enforcement response, language access, and the Haitian Response Initiative amid federal policy threats.
title: Committee on Finance
